desc:
Treacherous roots snake along the forest floor like long fingers from the bottom
of gnarled, blackened trees. Each trunk is covered with crusted yellow lichen
that clings to the dark bark, extending up into the lower boughs where darkness
masks the rest of the towering growth. The ground to the north and east climbs
sharply upward toward the mountains of the Emyn-nu-Fuin.
name: Climbing Forest

desc:
Jagged rocks protrude from the forest floor, jutting out of the soft soil
between large tree trunks. The woods are primarily made up of dark green and
black oaks, with a smattering of coniferous trees whose pine needles are
hardened by the harsher climate of the rising elevation.
name: Densely Forested Slope

desc:
Massive boulders rest at the bottom of a mountain valley, covered in green moss
and shrouded by the dark forest canopy above. Great black oaks loom ominously
over the tall stones, swaying to and fro in an unseen and unfelt breeze far away
from the dark stuffy air that blankets the valley floor.
name: Forest on a Mountain Slope

desc:
Hulking oak trees, ancient beyond reckoning, twist and turn between and around
each other in their rampant growth along rugged mountainous terrain. Massive
boughs grind together in their powerful struggle to force space in the crowded
forest, wrenching the already warped wood and squeezing it under immense
pressures for years untold. The old, compressed boughs appear almost shiny in
their ever-stressed but unrelenting struggle as they crush each other in the
darkness.
name: Twisted Mountain Forest

desc:
A dark, murky stream trickles and winds its way around great boulders as it
slowly descends the mountain slope into a forever-dark grove of menacing firs.
The tall, queer trees seem to feast on the filthy water as their roots extend
down into the rotten foul-smelling blackness. The roots themselves remain
covered in a thick layer of brown slime that extends to the soil and vegetation
surrounding them.
name: Black Brook in a Dark Mountainous Grove

desc:
Great round stones covered entirely in light-green crusted lichen rest deep in
the harsh earth on steep forested slopes. Tall oaks and firs loom over the
sunken boulders to create an eternally dark wasteland of hard cold stone and dry
cracked soil. Ragged and withered undergrowth barely cling to life amidst
parched dust under the choking forest canopy.
name: Boulder-strewn Forested Mountainside

desc:
A huge, forlorn tree grows amidst the mountains with its enormous branches
reaching high above the rest of the uneven canopy along the low peaks. The great
trunk plunges seemingly into the very stone of the mountain top, its titanic
roots cracking and splitting rock in every direction. The ground surrounding the
hulking tree is rugged with many boulders covering what little topsoil remains.
name: Massive Forlorn Tree

desc:
Large flat slabs of rock cover the slope that rises to the north. Gaps between
the stone create sudden explosions of tree growth. Great dark firs and oaks
appear rampant wherever breeches in the granite permit, their great boughs
reaching toward one another to block out the sky above.
name: Slab-covered Slope

desc:
The small mountaintop is dominated by burnt oak trees encircling a tiny glade
still entirely covered by intertwined, scorched branches. Both bark and bough
are charred to a uniform colour blacker than night itself. The forest seems to
hiss constantly from every direction, the air thick with malice and hostility.
name: Burned, Blackened Glade

desc:
A wide rocky cave has been turned into an orkish camp. Several charred pieces of
lumber lie against the northern wall of a cave, right next to a midden which is
littered with bones. A few large rocks frame the back of the cave, used as
makeshift seats next to a large cooking pot. The pot lies on its side towards
the back of the cave, its contents emptied.
name: Orkish Camp

desc:
Amidst hoary trees wounded with the hacking of axes lies a large, round stump, a
decaying remnant of a bygone era in the history of Mirkwood. Its dead roots
sprawl in every direction and all around the ground are scattered dark green
leaves. Even with meagre light, the girdle of oaks cast an imposing shadow on
the forest beyond. The dark surface of the trees struggle to reflect any light.
Webs stretch between the very tops of the trees, hanging ready for the return of
their masters.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Ancient oak trees with black bark stand here, timeless and still in the gloom of
Mirkwood. The thick forest-roof remains motionless as it imposes a suffocating
darkness upon that which it covers. Webs crawl up the trunks and weave among the
branches of the trees, burdening the canopy and further obscuring the roof of
the world. Amid rotten debris and long-fallen branches lies a hollow, ebony log
conquered by time and moss.
name: Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
The steep mountain slope is covered by great moss-covered boulders shrouded by
tall, darkened oaks and firs that grow in the spaces between the stones. Massive
branches and boughs produce a black blanket of dim leaves that blot out the
skies above a smothering ceiling of foliage.
name: Boulder-strewn Woodland

desc:
The evergreen trees here block out all possibility of sunlight penetrating the
forest canopy. Twisted trunks and sickly plants dot the forest floor, serving as
living examples of the vanquished vegetation in the eternal war of the plants as
they struggle for life in the forest of Mirkwood. Steep rocks and sharp stones
rise up to the southwest.
name: Shadowy Forest

desc:
Darkness reigns supreme in a low thickly-forested valley of the foothills of the
Emyn-nu-Fuin. Great trunks thrust themselves against one another as they reach
for light high above, squeezing hardened wood and rough bark alike under the
inhospitable pressures of continued growth filled with malice. Huge exposed
roots burst forth from the dry rocky ground in every direction only to plunge
back down into the hard unforgiving earth once again nearby, forming a twisted
lattice of precarious arms worn smooth by weather and the passage of time.
name: Dark Secluded Valley

desc:
Droplets of water drip from the cavern ceiling, shimmering as they trail down
thick webs laced across this tunnel. The spiders who wrought these webs seem to
have now forsaken this section of their domain, perhaps because of the moisture
that renders their silken snares useless. The floor is damp; it readily soaks up
the sprinkling water.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Fluffy white sacs hang suspended from strong webs stretching across a bulge in a
long tunnel. Several of the sacs are deflated and sway easily with the vibration
of the webs. Gossamer-thin strands of silk extend from these empty vessels, the
first weavings of freshly-hatched spiderlings. The walls of the chamber are
fluid with the motion of skittering spiders.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Elderly oak trees bent from many long years in Mirkwood lean with skin gnarled
and black, wrinkled from the ages. They creak at the joints, a sign of fatigue
from old age, yet the end of their vigil is nowhere in sight. This old forest
endures, or rather suffers, the great passage of time marching ever on and on.
name: Ancient Grove

desc:
An army of trees advance southwards to an incline, where the forest continues up
the slope. Arrayed among them are many tall oak and beech trees with gnarled
bark, obsidian in colour and as dark as the night. Long shadows creep beneath
the trees.
name: Black Forest before an Incline

desc:
Among many stony crags Mirkwood endures the rough, elevated terrain, dotting the
ground with trees of various heights. Detritus rings the larger boulders,
evidence of the elements working against these rocks over time. The mountain
sharply rises to the east and falls off in all other directions.
name: Crags above Mirkwood

desc:
These dark, corrupted trees are draped with the raiment of hoary webs, which
reach from branch to branch across the canopy as a reminder of the terror that
calls these old woods home. Roots twist about the sloping forest floor,
sprawling from the black trunks like long, crooked fingers, seeking sustenance.
name: Sloping Black Forest

desc:
It appears nobody has used this place for ages. Dwarven tools for the craft of
mining, caked with a thick layer of dust from years of disuse, are scattered
across many flat surfaces. From the heights of the cave many stalactites thrust
towards the ground like teeth, and in most places stalagmites reach upwards as
if to meet them. On an old track sits an empty cart of wood and iron. The track
and the passageway, braced along the rough walls with thick, wooden beams,
continues east. However, the way is blocked by collapsed rubble.
name: Forgotten Mineshaft

desc:
Among the fray of trees, the forest grows exceedingly thick, allowing only a
narrow passage west to east between old, hoary trunks and hanging limbs. The
ground is crowded with twisted roots that wind here and there as the soil
underfoot disappears almost entirely. Nevertheless, fern and forest weed manage
to find a way, growing within the cavities between the greedy roots.
name: Narrow Forest Passage

desc:
A makeshift shelter of dead logs that once leaned against an array of wide,
gnarled oaks has long since collapsed, abandoned and relinquished to the devices
of the forest. Ropy webs spread across the top of the ruined hut and climb the
trees up to the dark canopy. One of the trees bears the scratches of a large
animal, revealing the forest to be desolate but not uninhabited.
name: At a Ruined Shelter

desc:
Among the tall black oaks stand coarse, tall boulders as the ground forms a
slope leading up to the south. Between root and rock it is difficult to find a
path anywhere to the north or east. In the few places of clear ground, bracken
has taken up residence seeking all the light the canopy will allow to reach the
forest floor.
name: Boulders in the Forest

desc:
Rugged crags and large trees cover this part of the Emyn-nu-Fuin. The ground
slopes down to the north, allowing a descent to lower forests. The rough mix of
mountains and forest continues to the west and south. Tall firs dominate this
section of the mountain, but owing to the rocky turf, the forest is thin enough
to allow the sky to poke through the canopy.
name: Ascent of Trees

desc:
The terrain curves into a dry ravine travelling down from the north until it
comes to an impassable wall of trees. The cusp of the gully is lined with aspen,
while on the upward slopes west and east there are many beech and oak trees.
name: Gully in the Forest

desc:
Twisted oaks, some tall and some bent grow alongside one another, barely
allowing room for passage. Thick, strong webs wrap every possible surface, black
bark and green roof barely visible behind the dense network of ashen rope.
Matted dark green leaves are dispersed all about the ground.
name: Corrupted Forest

desc:
This area, sloping down to the south, is crowded with trees all the way up to
the southern cliff, over which some black oaks lean precariously, paying no mind
to gravity. The old forest intends to grow everywhere possible, as some defiant
saplings manage to jut out from the clumpy soil of the vertical face of the
cliff, their trunks curved straight out and upwards. The drop makes the way
south impassable, though it is not extremely far to the forest below.
name: Woods upon an Escarpment

desc:
The thickness of the dark forest is unhindered by the rugged terrain, which
climbs higher to the south. Maleficent shadows lurk beneath the eaves of these
wooded mountains, creeping over root, twig, and stone to cover the land in
darkness. Some black oaks grow around a giant, granite boulder that has been
sundered into two large pieces, the great work of the elements over time.
name: High Forest

desc:
A sea of trees can be seen over the edge of the cliff to the south, which stands
moderately high above the forest below, while on this level the woods are just
as thick. At the cliff the fresh air moves freely in stark contrast to the
stiffness that hangs in the area away from the cliff. This escarpment continues
to the east and the ground slopes upward to the north until it gradually levels
off.
name: Along a Forest Escarpment

desc:
The way through the mountains turns west and north as it winds through a small
gully. Many dark oaks follow the rough and bumpy passage, while the steep sides
are covered with bent, hanging plants and dark, clumpy soil. A few standing dead
trees loom overhead, ghosts of forest past. The rugged terrain breaks up the
forest, allowing many large holes in the canopy to appear.
name: Between Cliff and Bark

desc:
Barely more than hills among a forest, the heights of the mountains of Mirkwood
remain covered with its trees. At this elevation the forest has dissipated
enough for the canopy to allow light from above. The terrain grows harsh to the
south and west, while a faint game trail winds off to the east. A blanket of
rubble lines the mountainside to the north, completely hindering the growth of
vegetation, a rare occurrence in this old forest.
name: Mountains of Mirkwood

desc:
A slope rises from the south and onwards to the northwest, covered with jutting
crags and old, leaning oaks that brush up against one another, tangling their
branches and obstructing the passage of light into the forest. Stony hillocks
march along the incline, promising a rugged traversal for those who wish to
travel here. A singular beech stands amidst the hills, its knobby skin hiding
fallen needles in various grooves.
name: Corrugated Wooded Terrain

desc:
An array of trees with black, corrupted bark travels up the slope towards the
north. These ancient trunks seem heavy with the strain of their own limbs,
holding up the dark canopy which hangs in the stillness as if it were a sky
beneath a sky. The shadows are long and menacing here, deep in the dark forest.
name: Dark Forest Slope

desc:
Burrowed into the mountain, this cramped hovel serves as a guard post for the
forest lands claimed by the Durbuk-hai orcs. An aperture formed by a diagonal
crack in the soil to the south allows one to monitor the immediate vicinity. The
rough walls curve into an irregular ceiling, all layered with clumps of dirt.
The air is thick with moisture, and a steady dripping sound can be heard.
name: Durbuk-hai Refuge

desc:
Deep underground a plethora of crates and barrels containing provisions crowd
this tiny corridor of the cave. The containers are made of a crude wood, their
sides and tops bound and rivited with iron. The cavern floor is rough and worn
from frequent traffic in and out. All about the room a subterranean humidity
hangs densely in the air.
name: Dank Storeroom

desc:
Fallen oaks, husks of trees once old and weary, rest along the way north and
south. Even in an old forest nothing is wasted, as dark moss and fern grow among
these dead logs. In the distance the spectre of the forest can be seen in the
dark silhoutte of far off trees.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The ground falls and rises again forming a bowl dispersed with ancient beech and
oak. These timeworn trees appear to be touched by darkness; a strange sorcery
has perverted them and tinted their bark black like the raiment of ravens. Dense
foliage covers the ground, a dark green bracken that struggles to survive off
what meagre light the greater trees allow to reach them.
name: Depression in the Forest

desc:
All around but to the east the trees are impenetrable, as if the old forest is
jealous of its secrets and weary of sharing them with outsiders. A melee of
black branches with dark green leaves crowd the high areas, creating a thick
roof through which no light may pass. Even from the eagles Mirkwood protects its
hidden knowledge.
name: Edge of the Deep Forest

desc:
The trail extends west and east, its gravel worn from age and perturbed on the
edges by growing roots. To the north, the thick wall of trees gives way;
however, the imposing limbs of the forest trees still reach over the path, their
edifice forming a ceiling that denies any view of the sky.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Sinister branches crowd the area above and to the side of the trail, forming a
narrow corridor along which to travel. Many branches and leaves have fallen and
begun to decay, attracting a number of insects. Old, black roots dig into the
trail, disrupting the packed soil and layered gravel.
name: Narrow Old Forest Road

desc:
The trail, made of packed soil and layered with gravel, bends here, extending
east and south. The way to the south ends abruptly, blocked by dead logs and
branches, rotting beneath the eaves of the forest. This pile of debris, reaching
high into the tangled, black branches above, is no accident; its construction
appears purposeful and malevolent.
name: Obstruction in the Old Forest Road

desc:
Tall gnarled trees grow thick and close together, preventing all sunlight from
piercing the canopy above. Rotting vegetation covers the forest floor on top of
massive roots that extend from the feet of the dark trees. Huge rocks burst
forth from the ground in increasing numbers toward the south where the dark
woods continue up toward the feet of mountains.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The fir trees here are hoary and overgrown with sickly coloured fungi that vary
in their grotesque shapes and contours. Tangles of thick vines snake up along
the trees, twisting around boles and branches. To the south, the terrain becomes
elevated as the trees are forced to grow among boulders and rocks, while to the
east the forest floor appears to descend.
name: Tangled Woodland

desc:
The great bent trees here appear to be sparse amid a gloomy depression. The
majority of the trees are covered by a damp, grey moss that smells strongly of
decay. The forest to the north and west appears to climb away off into the
horizon, while a single dark and narrow gap opens to the south covered in thick
sticky webs.
name: Depression in a Dark Forest

desc:
Great dark oaks and firs grow close together with boughs and branches
intertwining in every direction. Thick twisted roots weave their way along the
forest floor in a tangled web of strangling creepers. The shadowy woods extend
to the north, south, and east beneath the blackness of the unseen treetops until
they collide with steep mountain cliffs.
name: Forested Valley

desc:
Black trees grow wildly and close together in soft soil covered with damp moss.
Small plants struggle to survive even in the fertile ground, deprived of
sunlight through the impregnable forest canopy looming high above. Small worn
stones covered with moss rest in the dirt beneath the trees, just above the
roots that visibly deform the ground.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Burrowing into the earth, this dark passageway is covered in unusually large
cobwebs. Sticky threads stretch taught forming layers of webbing around all but
the floor, which is covered in moist soil and some light vegetation that extends
from the forested depression to the north.
name: Webbed Recess

desc:
Thick, sticky webs cover every surface of the black tunnel. Nothing grows at
this depth along the hard tacky floor or on the milky white walls and ceiling.
The many tightly-woven strands of exceptionally strong silk are coated with a
clear liquid that clings immediately to anything that comes into contact with
it.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
The web-covered route through the earth is covered in what appears to be a
single, thick pale web that suffocates every surface. Occasionally, a light
breeze issues from above causing some of the loose fibers along the walls and
ceiling to sway to and fro. The slightest touch of the webs here causes a
visible tremor to carry on eastward into the foreboding darkness.
name: Bend in a Webbed Tunnel

desc:
A winding webbed corridor winds its way underground amidst numerous pillars,
carved from the elements and time, standing fast as if to hold open the pallid
veins of the earth. Stones of various sizes protrude from the walls but are
veiled by thick webs that stretch to conquer all visible surfaces.
name: Threaded Tunnel

desc:
A narrow corridor winds its way through the bottom of a large canyon that rises
to the south. Large white cracks are visible along the steep dark walls of rock.
The canyon floor is barren but for a few weeds. Far above, the edges of the
forest canopy can be seen hanging over the top of the recess like the roof of a
black tunnel.
name: Bottom of a Black Canyon

desc:
Dark black moss grows along the sides of the canyon while rotting debris, fallen
from the overhanging forest above, covers the ground. A lone crag juts from the
otherwise soft soiled wall to the south. Fern and weed fill the moist floor,
unhindered by the surrounding decay.
name: Wet, Humid Canyon

desc:
Black weeds sprout from the moist floor amidst piles of foul-smelling fungi and
rotting plant matter. Thick strands of ominous webbing stretch between the
looming stone walls, though the canopy above is still visible between them.
Drops of moisture collect along the bottoms of the silken ropes and occasionally
drip down on the dark mouldy piles below.
name: Bend in a Humid Canyon

desc:
Shallow pools of black water have formed in the soft dark soil to form a hidden
bog. Tall weeds grow thick in sloppy mud surrounding each pool amidst the
darkness in the still wet air. The trunk of a dead ancient oak leans
precariously against the west wall, its narrow top having already sunk down into
a particularly large black morass. A foreboding rounded tunnel entrance leads
into the eastern wall of the canyon revealing only complete and utter blackness
in that direction.
name: Swampy Canyon

desc:
Decayed vegetation covers the wet, muddy floor of the deep crevice. The steep
passage narrows into a jagged fissure and an apparent dead-end to the south.
Thick webs crawl up the cleavage of the canyon, making passage possible only by
those fell creatures that produced the sticky strands. Dark vines hang from the
forest over the top of the crack as they strain to reach the rancid water below.
name: Bog in the Canyon

desc:
A round tunnel has been bored into the stone, its walls and ceiling hidden by
thickly-woven webs. The wet stone floor seems to prevent silky strands from
sticking to it, in spite of the complex weave of tight cords that adorn the rest
of the tunnel. All surfaces save the floor are covered in thick white ropes that
glisten with some semi-translucent substance as well as clear moisture droplets
in the dark passageway.
name: Subterranean Passageway

desc:
Thick black mud and pools of dark water make up the floor of the tunnel even as
immense strands of sticky silk cords stretch tightly from wall to wall. Every
surface other than the floor is entirely white, covered with hideously strong
webs that jiggle and dance in a wavelike fashion when touched or jostled.
Strange claw-like impressions are clearly present in the mud along the floor,
and a foul reek seems to emanate from the north.
name: White Webbed Tunnel

desc:
An overwhelming stench permeates this horrid lair at the back of a wet
web-filled tunnel. Great bulbous egg sacs hang from the white woven ceiling
amidst a sticky mass of strong strands. Several shrunken husks of former victims
lay lifeless and shrivelled upon the wet stone floor, undoubtedly serving as
recent meals at the convenience of the creatures that have taken up residence
here. The roof is not visible through the thick webs spun from wall to wall,
masking unknown and foreboding heights that lurk above the countless silk
strands in the darkness.
name: Foul Nest

desc:
Rising high into the sky, imposing walls close in to form a narrow passage. Some
trees have managed to take root along the canyon floor in small clusters. The
space created by the fissure breaks up the forest canopy hanging far above. A
cleft looms ominously in the great stone wall to the west.
name: Steep Canyon

desc:
Uncounted stones fill the bottom of a steep incline, making it difficult for any
plant to take root in this area of the forest canyon. Rugged rock walls are
adorned with thick vegetation at the top of the crevice which sprouts dark vines
that hang down from the forest floor far above.
name: Rough Canyon

desc:
The cover of Mirkwood imposes itself over a canyon's narrow opening above as
branch and vine hang down ominously into the gaping chasm. The great dark walls
curve downward into a black soil as they form a narrow floor covered in dark
moss and fern growth.
name: Dark Canyon

desc:
Dark, ridged stone forms nearly vertical walls towering upward that block all
light from reaching this place. A cluster of gnarled oaks grows ravenously upon
one smooth area of soil, free from the loose rock and large boulders that
dominate most of the fissure's bottom. Loose stones and boulders give way to
dark moss and young ferns toward the east.
name: Sunless Chasm

desc:
A wide and deep crevice cleaving the hard bedrock beneath the soil of mirkwood
cuts its way down to form a steep slope toward the east. Debris made up of
decaying plant matter and cracked boulders lies piled here along the bottom of
the great crack in the earth, having fallen from far above over countless years
as it amassed to form an artificial floor.
name: Deep Fissure

desc:
A tiny recess in the stone carved by the elements, the rough ceiling of this
hollow provides some shelter from the open canyon to the east. Smooth grey walls
are disrupted by deep cracks that reveal veins of different types of rocks and
minerals. The floor is hard and barren but for a few grains of dirt.
name: Carved Hollow

desc:
Gnarled ancient trees grow evenly spaced inside a vast valley, with only the
tips of their leaves intertwining to form a matted mass of vegetation. Black
fungi twist around the serrated bark of the great trunks, keeping themselves
close to the moist air and under a cloak of permanent darkness.
name: Thickly Forested Valley

desc:
The endless rows of trees resemble a troupe of gnarled, dancing giants in the
dim light under the canopy. Massive roots snake out radially from grizzled
trunks while small clusters of vegetative growth sprout up amid red
umbrella-like mushrooms that dot the forest floor.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
Great evergreen trees grow closely together in a tangled labyrinth of boughs,
branches, and roots. The low branches are hard and dry, scraping and scratching
against one another from some unseen and unfelt wind far above. Dry stones
covered in yellow lichen lie scattered all about the forest floor.
name: Crowded Evergreen Forest

desc:
This small cave hollowed out beneath a thick patch of lichen-covered stones is
filled with organic debris of various sorts. Rotting vegetation is intermingled
with various bones that cover the cave floor. Old, dry wood from fallen trees
lines the stone walls and is visibly riddled with holes that appear to have been
bored throughout.
name: Small Cave

desc:
The forest floor descends steeply here, exposing large roots in the earthen
walls of a gully. Through dense foliage and weaving vines, the deep crack
transforms into a larger rift below the base of the forest. A small dark rivulet
steadily runs from above, forming a culvert along the centre of the path.
name: Gully Slope

desc:
Muddy walls form a dangerous looking overhang above a deep crevice that gouges
suddenly through the forest floor above. Trickling streams of water form small
puddles along the floor of the ravine as they steal trace amounts of mud and
silt. The small streams make distinct dripping sounds at various locations as
they wind their way down into the depths below.
name: Overhung Crevice

desc:
The wet stone walls enclosing this dark ravine are uneven and slippery as they
reach upward toward a narrow gap in the forest floor above. Strange slimy moulds
grow on the moist stone, partially disguising small remnants of old spider webs.
The deep crevice winds its way below Mirkwood Forest in a north-south direction,
while a large crack in the eastern wall reveals only empty darkness.
name: Deep Ravine

desc:
Steep slippery stone walls converge inward from above, narrowing to form a deep
crack hidden in the woods. A few dark roots crawl down the shiny wet walls,
greedily seeking more moisture in the depths below. What little light might have
pierced the treetops grows ever dimmer as the ravine sinks downward beneath the
forest floor.
name: Narrowing Gully

desc:
A great forested crack in the form of a gully suddenly ends in a collapsed pile
of mud and stone toward the east. Foul smelling moss grows atop the stones while
small mushrooms sprout from patches of moist soil spanning between great chunks
of mud and mangled roots.
name: Enclosed Gully

desc:
The walls of a large ravine grow wider near its top, making the trail that leads
upward precariously slippery. The winding path also continues west within the
rift, descending into the bowels of the forest. Dark gnarled roots twist around
the rubble-strewn floor while a damp, foul smelling moss covers the tops of the
rocks.
name: Ravine Opening

desc:
The cavernous walls of this steep ravine are heavily worn from erosion, a direct
result of water constantly dripping from the humid forest above. A great crevice
tears through the stone floor of the ravine like a gaping wound rent by some
mighty beast from a forgotten age. Complete darkness shrouds the depths of the
seemingly endless crack, though makeshift paths exist on either side allowing
passage east and west.
name: Bottom of a Ravine

desc:
Foliage begins to grow thick along the ravine floor as water trickling down from
the great twisting roots above makes its way into the soft soil. Slick green
moss covers the ground in uneven patches, producing a musty earthen smell. A
natural overhang has formed in the wall to the north, its exposed stone roof
glistening in the dark from the moisture droplets collecting on its surface.
name: Widening Ravine

desc:
Many dark brown and amber coloured layers of strata appear stacked atop one
another to form the walls of this tiny recess in the ravine to the south. The
belts of colour vary in thickness until, at the very top, all seem to meld into
a dome of beige stone. The alcove is dry but still smells of stifled humid air
and molding vegetation.
name: Layered Alcove

desc:
The tall jagged walls of the canyon race ever upward out of sight into looming
darkness. One side of the fissure towers over the other in a great precarious
overhang, obscuring any meagre glow of light that might have made its way
through the thick forest canopy. The great crack appears to grow shallower
toward the south, revealing the edges of a dark festering pool.
name: Covered Canyon

desc:
Stone rubble piled high along the base of the canyon walls suggests that seismic
activity once formed this great fissure. Moss-covered stones and black slimy
roots protrude from the steep exposed sides, even as a great dark pool of water
covered by a thick layer of indiscernible scum fills the deepest recesses of the
uneven floor.
name: Canyon Floor

desc:
The rough walls of a deep canyon widen as they ascend upward and are covered in
black moss growing thick on their exterior. Bone white roots weave in and out of
the soil, supporting the ancient trees of Mirkwood looming unseen above. A hole
gapes from the western wall while a deep fissure has formed in the eastern face.
Sticky-looking spiderwebs stretch between roots and rocks, too large to be meant
to snare insects.
name: Canyon Crossroads

desc:
The bottom of a deep crevice narrows from north to south amidst a warm, sticky
air that never seems to stir. With limited ground space available, numerous dark
weeds grow ever taller in the rich wet soil as they seek trace amounts of
indiscernible light from the meagre slit of visible forest canopy. An outline of
a large, decaying log can be seen high above, having fallen across the crack in
the earth.
name: Humid Narrow Crevice

desc:
Crumbling dried husks drained of life cover the floor of a shallow pit that
reeks of death. Thin crispy flakes of dried skin cracked off from devoured
corpses create a waste-deep pile of horrific refuse, the leftover remains of a
large arachnid's meals piled up over the years. The latest remains lay on top of
the pile, and some are still recognisable by their dried-out goblin eyes, frozen
wide in a look of terror as they were devoured in their last concious moments.
name: Stinking Pile of Refuse

desc:
Wretched boughs blackened by forces unknown twist and warp around each other and
their neighbouring tree trunks amidst a great glade that ever remains as dark as
a moonless night. A sense of great hate and vengeance lingers in the stuffy
still air beneath the baleful branches, which regularly emit foreboding groaning
noises and occasionally produce a loud cracking sound with no visible rhyme or
reason.
name: Baleful Blackened Glade
